communicative_purpose: Formal act of submitting application for employment
trigger_situations: When applying for jobs, responding to job postings, career development
contexts: Professional settings, job search, human resources contexts
variations: poser sa candidature, envoyer sa candidature, faire acte de candidature
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'déposer sa candidature' which is more formal; using wrong reflexive form
collocates: poser sa candidature, j'ai posé ma candidature, soumettre sa candidature
example_2: Tu peux dire poser sa candidature quand tu veux exprimer to apply for a position.
example_3: On utilise poser sa candidature dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can say apply when you want to express to apply for a position.
example_3_translation: We use applying in everyday life.

communicative_purpose: Indicating possession of professional or practical experience
trigger_situations: During job interviews, describing qualifications, writing CV
contexts: Professional contexts, job applications, career discussions
variations: avoir de l'expérience, posséder de l'expérience, bénéficier de l'expérience
collocates: avoir de l'expérience, beaucoup d'expérience, expérience professionnelle
example_2: Tu peux dire avoir de l'expérience quand tu veux exprimer to have experience.
example_3: On utilise avoir de l'expérience dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can say have experience when you want to express to have experience.
example_3_translation: We use having experience in daily life.

communicative_purpose: Indicating lack of employment, being without a job
trigger_situations: During job search, when explaining not working, in statistics contexts
contexts: Social discussions, statistics, job center contexts
variations: être au chômage, être sans emploi, être au RSA (French specific)
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'chômé' meaning temporarily not working; using wrong preposition
collocates: être au chômage, depuis combien de temps au chômage, le chômage
example_2: Tu peux dire être au chômage quand tu veux exprimer to be unemployed.
example_3: On utilise être au chômage dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can say be unemployed when you mean to be unemployed.
example_3_translation: We use being unemployed in everyday life.

communicative_purpose: Discussing terms and conditions to reach agreement
trigger_situations: During business deals, employment negotiations, service agreements
contexts: Business settings, legal contexts, professional negotiations
variations: négocier un contrat, signer un contrat, rompre un contrat
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'négocier un prix' (negotiate a price); using wrong verb
collocates: négocier un contrat, avons négocié,有能力 négocier
example_2: Tu peux dire négocier un contrat quand tu veux exprimer to negotiate a contract.
example_3: On utilise négocier un contrat dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can say negotiate a contract when you mean to negotiate a contract.
example_3_translation: We use negotiating a contract in everyday life.

communicative_purpose: Indicating that someone demonstrates a quality or characteristic
trigger_situations: During evaluations, describing behavior, in professional contexts
contexts: Workplace evaluations, character references, professional assessments
collocates: faire preuve de, preuves de, fait preuve de professionnalisme
example_2: Tu peux dire faire preuve de quand tu veux exprimer to demonstrate/to show evidence of.
example_3: On utilise faire preuve de dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can say to demonstrate when you want to express to demonstrate/to show evidence of.
example_3_translation: We use to demonstrate in daily life.

communicative_purpose: Describing the process of choosing between options
trigger_situations: During management roles, in strategic planning, in personal life
variations: prendre des décisions, décider, arrêter une décision
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'décider de' (decide on); using wrong verb form
collocates: prendre des décisions, décisions stratégiques,有能力 prendre des décisions
example_2: Tu peux dire prendre des décisions quand tu veux exprimer to make decisions.
example_3: On utilise prendre des décisions dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can say make decisions when you mean to make decisions.
example_3_translation: We use making decisions in daily life.

communicative_purpose: Indicating advancement to higher position in organization
trigger_situations: During career development, after good performance, in corporate contexts
variations: être promu, obtenir une promotion, être élevé en grade
common_mistakes: Confusing passive with active form; using 'promouvoir' incorrectly
collocates: être promu, il a été promu, promu au poste de
example_2: Tu peux dire être promu quand tu veux exprimer to be promoted.
example_3: On utilise être promu dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can say be promoted when you want to express to be promoted.
example_3_translation: We use to be promoted in daily life.

communicative_purpose: Formal act of leaving a job voluntarily
trigger_situations: When changing jobs, leaving a toxic work environment, retirement
variations: démissionner, quitter son poste, donner sa démission
collocates: démissionner, il a démissionné, envie de démissionner
example_2: Tu peux dire démissionner quand tu veux exprimer to resign/to quit.
example_3: On utilise démissionner dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can say resign when you mean to resign/to quit.
example_3_translation: We use resign in everyday life.

communicative_purpose: Describing working in high-stress environment with deadlines
trigger_situations: During stressful work periods, in demanding jobs, in crisis situations
variations: travailler sous pression, être sous pression, faire face à la pression
common_mistakes: Confusing preposition 'sous' with 'dans'; using wrong article
collocates: travailler sous pression, beaucoup de pression, pression de travail
example_2: Tu peux dire travailler sous pression quand tu veux exprimer to work under pressure.
example_3: On utilise travailler sous pression dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can say work under pressure when you mean to work under pressure.
example_3_translation: We use working under pressure in daily life.

meaning: to earn one's living/to make a living
communicative_purpose: Describing earning money to support oneself
trigger_situations: When discussing employment, during career talks, in financial contexts
contexts: Personal finance, career discussions, family conversations
variations: gagner sa vie, subvenir à ses besoins, subvenir à ses besoins
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'gagner du temps' (save time); using wrong article
collocates: gagner sa vie, bien gagner sa vie, gagne-pain
example_2: Tu peux dire gagner sa vie quand tu veux exprimer to earn one's living/to make a living.
example_3: On utilise gagner sa vie dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can say earn one's living when you mean to earn one's living/to make a living.
example_3_translation: We use earning a living in everyday life.

communicative_purpose: Indicating possession of required skills or abilities
trigger_situations: During evaluations, job interviews, skill assessments
variations: être compétent, être qualifié, avoir des compétences
common_mistakes: Confusing with English meaning; using with wrong noun gender
collocates: être compétent, très compétent, compétent dans son domaine
example_2: Tu peux dire être compétent quand tu veux exprimer to be competent/to be skilled.
example_3: On utilise être compétent dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can say be competent when you want to express to be competent/to be skilled.
example_3_translation: We use being competent in daily life.

communicative_purpose: Designated time off for new parents to care for child
trigger_situations: After childbirth, adoption, when caring for new baby
contexts: Workplace policies, family discussions, HR contexts
variations: congé parental, congé maternité (mothers only), congé paternité (fathers only)
common_mistakes: Confusing types of leave; not knowing French-specific terminology
collocates: congé parental, prendre un congé parental, durée du congé parental
example_2: Tu peux dire congé parental quand tu veux exprimer parental leave.
example_3: On utilise congé parental dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can say parental leave when you want to express parental leave.
example_3_translation: We use parental leave in everyday life.

communicative_purpose: Describing working beyond normal scheduled hours
trigger_situations: During busy periods, to meet deadlines, in demanding jobs
variations: faire des heures supplémentaires, faire des heures supplémentaires, faire du rab' (informal)
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'faire des heures' alone; using wrong adjective
collocates: faire des heures supplémentaires, heures supplémentaires payées, trop d'heures supplémentaires
example_2: Tu peux dire faire des heures supplémentaires quand tu veux exprimer to work overtime.
example_3: On utilise faire des heures supplémentaires dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can say work overtime when you mean to work overtime.
example_3_translation: We use working overtime in daily life.

communicative_purpose: Indicating scheduled meeting to evaluate candidacy
trigger_situations: During job search, during evaluation periods, in professional settings
contexts: Human resources, job search, professional meetings
variations: avoir un entretien, passer un entretien, être convoqué à un entretien
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'entretien' meaning maintenance; using wrong article
collocates: avoir un entretien, entretien d'embauche, préparer un entretien
example_2: Tu peux dire avoir un entretien quand tu veux exprimer to have an interview.
example_3: On utilise avoir un entretien dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can say have an interview when you want to express to have an interview.
example_3_translation: We use having an interview in daily life.

meaning: to take a leave of absence (congé)s
communicative_purpose: Designated time away from work for specific reasons
trigger_situations: During sabbatical, for personal reasons, for extended illness
contexts: Workplace, human resources, formal leave requests
variations: poser un LAP, prendre un congé sans solde, être en LAP
common_mistakes: Confusing with other types of leave; not knowing the acronym meaning
collocates: poser un LAP, LAP demandé, durée du LAP
example_2: Tu peux dire poser un LAP quand tu veux exprimer to take a leave of absence (congé)s.
example_3: On utilise poser un LAP dans la vie quotidienne.
example_2_translation: You can say put a LAP when you want to express to take a leave of absence (congé)s.
example_3_translation: We use a LAP in everyday life.
